tex:
(a) 
The table shows some information about the opening hours of a café.
\newline

The café opens 4 days a week.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Day & Opening time & Closing time & Number of hours open \\
\hline
Thursday & $8\,\text{am}$ & $4.30\,\text{pm}$ & \\
\hline
Friday & $8.30\,\text{am}$ & & $7\frac{1}{2}$ \\
\hline
Saturday & $9.30\,\text{am}$ & $5.30\,\text{pm}$ & 8 \\
\hline
Sunday & & $3.30\,\text{pm}$ & 5 \\
\hline
& & Total number of hours open & 29 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Complete the table.
\newline

t
(i)  A waiter works 29 hours a week in the café.
\newline

He is paid \$9.50 per hour.
\newline

He is paid for 52 weeks of the year.
\newline

Work out his total pay for the year.
\newline

 The chef is paid 32\% more than the waiter per hour.
\newline

Work out how much the chef is paid per hour.
\newline

Here is part of the café's menu.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|}
\hline
MENU \\
Cup of coffee \quad \$2.50 \qquad Slice of pizza   \$3.70 \\
Cup of tea \quad \$2.30 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Raj buys 2 cups of coffee, 1 cup of tea and 3 slices of pizza.
\newline

Calculate the change he receives from \$20.
\newline

The chef records the types of baguettes the café sells in one day.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
salad & cheese & salad & salad & egg & cheese & cheese & salad & cheese & egg & salad \\
\hline
cheese & salad & salad & egg & cheese & salad & salad & egg & salad & cheese & salad \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Complete the frequency table to show this information.
\newline

You may use the tally column to help you.
\newline

\hline
Type of baguette & Tally & Frequency \\
\hline
Cheese & & \\
\hline

\hline
Salad & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

 On the grid, draw a bar chart to show this information.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Day & Opening time & Closing time & Number of hours open \\
\hline
Thursday & 8\,am & 4.30\,pm & \\
\hline
Friday & 8.30\,am & & $7\frac{1}{2}$ \\
\hline
Saturday & 9.30\,am & 5.30\,pm & 8 \\
\hline
Sunday & & 3.30\,pm & 5 \\
\hline
& & Total number of hours open & 29 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

Complete the table.
\newline

\newline

He is paid \$9.50 per hour.

tex:
(a) 
Manjit asks 30 students whether they prefer joke books, puzzle books or poetry books.
\newline

The results are shown in the table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Type of book & Number of students & Pie chart sector angle \\
\hline
Joke & 8 & \\
\hline
Puzzle & 18 & \\
\hline
Poetry & 4 & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Complete the table.
\newline

 Complete the pie chart.
\newline

 One of the students is chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that this student prefers puzzle books.
\newline

The stem-and-leaf diagram shows the test scores for 24 students.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{c|c c c c c c}
2 & 2 & 5 & 6 & 9 & & \\
3 & 3 & 7 & 8 & & & \\
4 & 2 & 3 & 5 & 5 & 7 & 8 \\
5 & 1 & 1 & 1 & 5 & 6 & 8   9 \\
6 & 0 & 2 & 5 & 7 & & \\
\end{tabular}

Key: $4 \mid 2$ represents 42
(i)  Write down the mode.
\newline

 75\% of the 24 students pass the test.
\newline

Work out the lowest score needed to pass the test.
\newline

 Work out the range.
\newline

 Frankie was absent on the day of the test.
\newline

His score is not on the stem-and-leaf diagram.
\newline

When he takes the test, his score increases the range by 3 marks.
\newline

Write down the two possible values of Frankie's score.
\newline

{---}]

content.content:
Manjit asks 30 students whether they prefer joke books, puzzle books or poetry books.
\newline

The results are shown in the table.

Complete the table.
\newline

Complete the pie chart.
\newline

One of the students is chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that this student prefers puzzle books.
\newline

Key: $4 \mid 2$ represents 42
\end{center}

Write down the mode.
\newline

75\% of the 24 students pass the test.

\newline

\newline

\newline

Write down the two possible values of Frankie's score.

tex:

\newline

 Work out the amount of flour, butter and sugar needed to make 50 biscuits.

butter 

sugar

One sack of flour makes a maximum of  loaves of bread.

The amount of flour left over is  g.

\newline

butter \AnswerLine[][g]

sugar \AnswerLine[][g]

\newline

tex:
(a) 
Write 6479 correct to the nearest 100.
\newline

Write down the multiple of 13 that is between 100 and 110.
\newline

Find the reciprocal of 0.6.
\newline

Work out.
\newline
\[3 + 4 \times 2\]

Write down an irrational number with a value between 15 and 20.
\newline

By writing each number in the calculation correct to 1 significant figure, find an estimate for the value of
\[\frac{423.8 - 78.4}{23.5}.
\newline
\]

You must show all your working.
\newline

tex:
The scale drawing shows the positions of three towns, $R$, $S$ and $T$, on a map.
\newline

$RS$ and $ST$ are straight roads between the towns.
\newline

The scale is 1 centimetre represents 8 kilometres.
\newline

(a) 
Work out the actual distance between $R$ and $S$.
\newline

Another town, $V$, is on a bearing of $163°$ from $R$ and on a bearing of $215°$ from $T$.
\newline

Mark the position of $V$ on the map.
\newline

A man cycles at a constant speed of $24\,\text{km/h}$ along the straight road from $S$ to $T$.
\newline

After 1 hour and 50 minutes he stops at a café, $C$.
\newline

Mark the position of $C$ on the map.
\newline

You must show all your working.
\newline

A hotel, $H$, is on a bearing of $321°$ from $R$.
\newline

Work out the bearing of $R$ from $H$.
\newline

Write the scale $1\,\text{cm}$ to $8\,\text{km}$ in the form $1 : n$.
\newline

qtype: finding nth term of a visual sequence
tex:
(a) 
t

(i)  Write down the order of rotational symmetry of the diagram.
\newline

 On the diagram, draw all the lines of symmetry.
\newline

The grid shows the first three diagrams in a sequence.
\newline

Each diagram is made using small grey and small white squares to make grey and white columns.
\newline

(i)  On the grid, draw Diagram 4.
\newline

(b) Complete this statement.
\newline

Diagram $n$ has  grey columns.
\newline

(c) Find an expression, in terms of $n$, for the total number of columns in Diagram $n$.
\newline

(d) Find an expression, in terms of $n$, for the fraction of columns that are grey in Diagram $n$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Diagram number & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 \\
\hline
Number of grey squares & 6 & 8 & 10 & & \\
\hline
Number of white squares & 3 & 8 & 15 & & \\
\hline
Total number of squares & 9 & 16 & 25 & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) Complete the table.
\newline

(b) Write an expression, in terms of $n$, for the number of grey squares in Diagram $n$.
\newline

(c) The number of white squares in Diagram $n$ is $n(n + 2)$.
\newline

Work out the number of white squares in Diagram 30.
\newline

(d) Diagram $k$ has a **total** of 1296 squares.
\newline

Work out the value of $k$.
\newline
